claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care

Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - Alex, who is 6 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 6 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, there are some specific circumstances where you may still be able to claim Child Benefit.

**Which country are you currently living in, and are you or your partner a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of the armed forces posted abroad)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not a Crown servant, and neither is my partner. We're just ordinary citizens living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. 

**Which country are you currently living in?** This is important because Child Benefit may be available if you're living in certain countries (such as some EEA countries or countries with specific agreements with the UK).

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not say the specific country, but it's not one of those special agreement countries. Does that affect whether I can claim?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Unfortunately, yes, it does affect your eligibility.

Based on what you've told me, since you're living abroad in a country that doesn't have a special agreement with the UK, and neither you nor your partner are Crown servants, you would not be eligible for Child Benefit.

According to the rules, you can only get Child Benefit if you live in the UK, unless you go to live in certain countries with special agreements or you're a Crown servant posted abroad.

Let me provide my formal assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex** because you don't meet the residence requirement. While Alex meets the age criteria (under 16), the fundamental requirement is that you normally must live in the UK to qualify for Child Benefit, unless specific exceptions apply (living in certain countries with agreements or being a Crown servant), which don't apply in your case.

If your circumstances change and you move back to the UK in the future, you would be able to claim Child Benefit at that time.

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16). However, the claimant is living abroad in a country that does not have a special agreement with the UK, and neither the claimant nor their partner are Crown servants. Under the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify only if you live in the UK, unless you go to live in certain countries or are a Crown servant. Since neither of these exceptions apply, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child aged 6, but is living abroad in a country without a special agreement with the UK and is not a Crown servant. The residence requirement for Child Benefit is not met, so they are not eligible.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 6 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16). However, the claimant is living abroad in a country that does not have a special agreement with the UK, and neither the claimant nor their partner are Crown servants. Under the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify only if you live in the UK, unless you go to live in certain countries or are a Crown servant. Since neither of these exceptions apply, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child aged 6, but is living abroad in a country without a special agreement with the UK and is not a Crown servant. The residence requirement for Child Benefit is not met, so they are not eligible.
